2603.29094.title: Evaluating a Data-Driven Redesign Process for Intelligent Tutoring Systems
2603.29094.paper_summary: Past research has defined a general process for the data-driven redesign of educational technologies and has shown that in carefully-selected instances, this process can help make systems more effective. In the current work, we test the generality of the approach by applying it to four units of a middle-school mathematics intelligent tutoring system that were selected not based on suitability for redesign, as in previous work, but on topic. We tested whether the redesigned system was more effective than the original in a classroom study with 123 students. Although the learning gains did not differ between the conditions, students who used the Redesigned Tutor had more productive time-on-task, a larger number of skills practiced, and greater total knowledge mastery. The findings highlight the promise of data-driven redesign even when applied to instructional units *not* selected as likely to yield improvement, as evidence of the generality and wide applicability of the method.
2603.29094.paper_summary_zh: 過去的研究定義了一個基於數據的教育技術重新設計的一般過程，並顯示在精心選擇的情況下，這一過程可以幫助使系統更有效。在目前的研究中，我們通過將其應用於四個中學數學智能輔導系統的單元來測試該方法的一般性，這些單元的選擇不是基於重新設計的適用性，如以往的研究，而是基於主題。我們測試了重新設計的系統在與123名學生的課堂研究中是否比原始系統更有效。儘管學習增益在不同條件之間沒有差異，但使用重新設計輔導系統的學生在任務時間上更具生產力，練習的技能數量更多，且總知識掌握程度更高。這些發現突顯了基於數據的重新設計的潛力，即使在應用於未被選為可能產生改進的教學單元時，這也證明了該方法的一般性和廣泛適用性。
2603.29094.author: Qianru Lyu et.al.
2603.29094.authors: Qianru Lyu, Conrad Borchers, Meng Xia, Karen Xiao, Paulo F. Carvalho, Kenneth R. Koedinger, Vincent Aleven
